danger_signals.visual_form: Super deformed monster sprites with exaggerated hostile poses
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring avoidance or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Damaging water, floor holes, falling objects (buckets), fire rooms
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-specific danger zones that reduce health on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Isolated arena with dramatically larger enemy sprite; changed background palette
Interior mansion/cabin/pyramid rooms with limited visibility cues
danger_signals.meaning: High-tension horror atmosphere; unpredictable enemy spawn zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Human-shaped sprites in robes with distinct animation patterns

safety_signals.meaning: Collectible that increases health bar permanent capacity via XP system

safety_signals.visual_form: Axe (default) and shotgun sprites with limited ammo indication
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary damage increase or ranged capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Door/exit portal at end of level sequence
Glowing orb sprites placed in hidden/optional locations
safety_signals.meaning: Secret unlock trigger; safe to collect without penalty

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pumpkin King as primary boss; robed cultists as minions; skull imagery in Hell House stage
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cultist robed forms; Pumpkin King boss sprite; masked antagonists
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell House stage with occult atmosphere; cult ritual chambers; skull decorations; pyramid temple (Egypt stage)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Michael Jackson-inspired vampire (Thriller reference); werewolf; possessed girl; flying knives; spiders; Fly boss; chestburster alien parody
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each stage features distinct monster types (flying enemies, ground-based, boss variants)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Thematic stage design: Diamond Lake (aquatic), Devil Town (gothic), Diamond Camp (cabins with screaming paintings), Sewer (gross), Hell House (demonic)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character with mask (revived from grave); associated with axe weapon
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rick appears in mask form as default state; unmasked in ending cinematics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: West Mansion as origin point; appears throughout all stages; associated with Jennifer (goal NPC)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jennifer weeping at Rick's grave; lightning strike animation; grave revivification cutscene
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rick emerges with mask equipped; Pumpkin King spawns in adjacent grave; Jennifer captured

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Platforming environments with scattered enemies; background art reflects location theme (mansion interior, forest, pyramid)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol or spawn on player approach; collectibles remain stationary; XP counter visible at top of HUD

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arena isolation; enemy sprite enlarged; dramatic pose; music change implied by source data
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-specific attack patterns; no enemy respawn from sides; arena bounds enforced

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health bar visual reduction; HUD flashing or color change implied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player knockback increased; movement speed potentially affected; one hit from certain hazards = death

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extended ending cinematics unlock; epilogue images displayed (Rick + Jennifer on hill; West Mansion approach)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Altered narrative conclusion; ominous foreshadowing of prequel-to-original-game connection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Director office reveal; mask removal cutscene; credits roll; mask comes alive showing true threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story subversion: 'all was movie' twist; mask animation suggesting deeper antagonist

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Epilogue image: Rick and Jennifer approaching West Mansion in storm; ominous text overlay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay events recontextualized as premonition; links to original Splatterhouse premise

environmental_storytelling.example: Diamond Lake stage uses aquatic/flying enemies; Sewer stage has scatological design language; Hell House features occult imagery and chainsaws; Pyramid has trapdoors
Gothic Devil Town vs. comedic Cabin interiors vs. hellish final stage; color grading communicates danger escalation
Grave revival opening; director office ending; cultist kidnapping scene; screaming paintings in cabin; masked antagonist reveal
Sewer labeled 'BE GARBAGE OF CESSPOOL'; fire room creates claustrophobic threat; falling buckets in cabin = comedic horror; trapdoors in pyramid = ancient-trap trope
Crystal balls hidden in optional locations; unlocks extended epilogue suggesting prequel canon; rewards exploration beyond main path
Michael Jackson Thriller vampire boss; alien chestburster parody; werewolf boss; possessed girl trope; communicates horror-comedy genre blend

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Kidnaps Jennifer; forces Rick through multi-stage gauntlet; revealed as movie actor in subversion; mask animates post-defeat showing true threat
Boss-tier encounter in Devil Town; drags Rick into skull death scene in Hell House; poses distinct threat from common monsters
Fly boss (Stage 3); Werewolf (Stage 5); appear once per stage; arena-isolated; visual/audio cues signal defeat progression

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Common enemies (flying knives, spiders, hands, bats)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spawn from arena sides; patrol preset paths; respawn on player defeat; no individual behavioral variation

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Activated by proximity or stage logic
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Deal damage on contact; cannot be defeated; force navigation around or through hazard zones
Appears in opening cutscene (weeping); referenced as rescue objective; appears in ending cinematics and epilogue; passive non-combatant
Revived from grave at game start; wields axe as primary weapon; can equip shotgun (limited ammo); health bar grows via XP enemy kills; acts and performs for director in meta-twist ending

notes: Splatterhouse: Wanpaku Graffiti uses super-deformed aesthetic to undercut horror genre tropes, creating cognitive dissonance that drives dark comedy. Visual communication relies on stage-environment theming, sprite exaggeration, and cinematic cutscenes rather than realistic danger indicators. World-state shifts are explicitly signaled through pre-stage intros (Base 00 data) and narrative cutscenes. The game's core manifestation layer communicates through: (1) Enemy roster variety = location identity; (2) Hazard placement = progression gates; (3) Boss arena isolation = threat escalation; (4) Collectible crystals = meta-narrative unlock; (5) Ending subversion = story recontextualization. Political/factional elements are minimal—primarily Cultist antagonism vs. Rick protagonist. The game functions as prequel framing via environmental and narrative signaling (West Mansion callbacks, Jennifer kidnapping echo).
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - debug/level data)
